metadata.seed: Total fuckup My depression got so bad again that I dropped out of university. I tried talking to my teachers but they told me I just didn’t try hard enough. I tried so hard every day but my best is not enough it seems. My mom yelled at me about how I’m throwing away my future. Everybody thinks I’m just a waste of space. Fucking up one thing to the next. All I’m good for is making others feel miserable. Why should I even be here anymore.

patient.query: Total fuckup My depression got so bad again that I dropped out of university. I tried talking to my teachers but they told me I just didn’t try hard enough. I tried so hard every day but my best is not enough it seems. My mom yelled at me about how I’m throwing away my future. Everybody thinks I’m just a waste of space. Fucking up one thing to the next. All I’m good for is making others feel miserable. Why should I even be here anymore.

llm_response.response:
I am so incredibly sorry you're going through this—it sounds like you're in immense pain, and dropping out of university while battling depression is a huge deal. It's truly disheartening that your teachers were dismissive and unhelpful, and your mom's reaction likely just added to the overwhelming pressure you're feeling. Please know that your worth isn't defined by academic success or anyone else's expectations, and those feelings of being a waste of space are a direct result of your depression talking, not a reflection of reality. 

It takes immense strength to even reach out and share what you're experiencing, and I want you to know that there are people who care and want to help you navigate this—please consider reaching out to a crisis hotline or mental health professional, as they can provide support and guidance during this difficult time. You deserve kindness and understanding, especially from yourself.
